_meta.purpose: Static, hand-compiled Sydney Basin VFR reporting point / landmark dataset for the VFR NAVLOG Trainer (HSC Software Engineering sample PWA).
_meta.disclaimer: Training/demonstration data only. NOT for real navigation or flight planning. Positions are approximate (general-knowledge/public-source coordinates for the landmark, not surveyed VFR reporting-point coordinates) and route descriptions are simplified from public ERSA-derived guidance. Always check the current ERSA/VTC and NOTAMs before any real flight.
_meta.compiledFrom: Public, non-restricted reference material — general Sydney Basin geography, place-name coordinate lookups, and route mentions from ERSA-derived Bankstown aerodrome-chart extracts and CASA's public "Stay OnTrack: Flying the Sydney Region" guidance. No live or licensed CASA/Airservices data feed is used anywhere in this app.
_meta.schemaNote: nearAerodromes lists ICAO codes from data/aerodromes.json that this waypoint is commonly used as a reporting/turning point for — this is how the Nav Page filters "waypoints near this aerodrome", and how later stages (Route Planner) will offer waypoint suggestions when a route's departure/arrival aerodrome is picked.

waypoints.name: Prospect Reservoir
waypoints.notes: Large, unmistakable water body used as a VFR join/turning point west of Bankstown; also a common training-area reference for routes between Bankstown and Camden.

waypoints.name: Warragamba Dam (Lake Burragorang)
waypoints.notes: Sydney's main water-supply dam, a very prominent landmark west of Camden. Terrain rises quickly toward the Blue Mountains just beyond it — a natural "turn back" reference for training routes rather than a through-point.

waypoints.name: Parramatta CBD
waypoints.notes: First leg of the standard Bankstown-to-north track, continuing via Pennant Hills and Hornsby. Built-up area — noise abatement applies, don't linger overhead.

waypoints.name: Pennant Hills
waypoints.notes: Waypoint on the Bankstown-to-north track, between Parramatta CBD and Hornsby.

waypoints.name: Hornsby (railway workshops)
waypoints.notes: Decision point on the Bankstown-to-north track: once north of here, routes typically continue either to the coast or on toward Patonga/the Central Coast.

waypoints.name: Patonga
waypoints.notes: Coastal village near the Hawkesbury River mouth; a common northern coastal reporting point on routes from Bankstown toward Warnervale/the Central Coast.

waypoints.name: Brooklyn (Hawkesbury River)
waypoints.notes: Distinctive river-mouth/bridge landmark just south of Patonga, useful visual reference for the coastal track into Warnervale.

waypoints.name: Nepean River (Menangle)
waypoints.notes: River-valley landmark south of Camden, useful as a lead-in feature for routes toward Picton, Wedderburn and The Oaks.

waypoints.name: Appin
waypoints.notes: Township waypoint near Broughton Pass/Cataract, south of Campbelltown — a common marker on routes heading further south toward Wollongong or the Southern Highlands.

waypoints.name: Campbelltown
waypoints.notes: Large, easily identified town south-west of Sydney — good general visual reference between Camden, Wedderburn and The Oaks.

waypoints.name: Picton
waypoints.notes: Township south of Camden on the track toward the Southern Highlands/Wollongong; also near The Oaks.

waypoints.name: Royal National Park (Waterfall)
waypoints.notes: Coastal escarpment/national park landmark on the Sydney Basin-to-Wollongong track — sits directly under/near the southern edge of the Sydney Class C shelf, worth extra airspace awareness.

waypoints.name: Bald Hill (Stanwell Park)
waypoints.notes: Well-known coastal lookout just north of Wollongong — a popular VFR coastal join point for arrivals into Wollongong from the north.

waypoints.name: Mittagong
waypoints.notes: Southern Highlands township on the track between the Sydney Basin and Goulburn — useful mid-route turning/decision point on the longer cross-country legs.
